position_summary: Claude's position has the right product thesis, but the artifact partially contradicts it: the proposed arc says falsification is the moat while delaying falsification until scenes 3-4 after funnel and YES. Repo verification also shows the proposed Streamlit UI is feasible only if it handles the real `referee_triple` contract, which is not the simplified 4-hop YES/UNTESTED/REFUTED surface described in the plan.

findings.title: The arc claims falsification-first, but the scene order does not lead with falsification
findings.evidence: The preserve-intent thesis says the strongest demo leads with the confident NO and UNTESTED catch, but the plan orders Scene 1 funnel, Scene 2 NAB2 YES, Scene 3 UNTESTED, Scene 4 REFUTED at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:82-95`. Claude's own opening says the arc is 'leading with the funnel then the verdict range.'
findings.concern_path: A judge sees a conventional feature/results tour for the first half of a 180s video, then the product's differentiator arrives late. This sands down the novel claim without explicitly arguing against it, and weakens Demo + Impact because the 'willingness to refute is the product' is not the opening visual proof.
findings.suggested_action: Force Claude to either defend the current order with judge-impact reasoning, or revise the arc so the first live interaction is SATB1 UNTESTED or SLC1A5 REFUTED before the NAB2 YES/funnel context.

findings.title: The Streamlit wrapper is not a simple 4-hop verdict renderer for all hero cases
findings.evidence: The plan promises a '4-hop receipt chain' at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:61`, but `referee_triple` returns only HOP-0 when the knockdown gate fails: `src/arbiter/lbd/referee_triple.py:103-106`. Verified live: SATB1/asthma returns `answer='untested'`, `hops=1`. The notebook output also shows only HOP 0 for SATB1 at `notebooks/pyzobot_arbiter_evidence_chain.ipynb:328-336`.
findings.concern_path: A thin UI that assumes four hop cards will either show misleading empty downstream hops for the UNTESTED hero case, or visually collapse to one card and make the receipt-chain promise look weaker than narrated. This is a direct demo-surface correctness risk, not polish.
findings.suggested_action: Specify the UI contract branch: gate-failed triples stop at HOP-0 with an explicit 'downstream hops not interpretable' visual, while supported/refuted-for-C triples render four hops.

findings.title: The plan's `answer` enum is incomplete and can mislabel real outputs
findings.evidence: Claude lists `answer` as supported / untested / refuted_for_c / supported_weak / supported_flagged, but the implementation can also return `untested_for_c`, `refuted_effect`, and `refuted_program` at `src/arbiter/lbd/referee_triple.py:121-132`. The sweep contains `refuted_effect: 1` in `data/lbd_out/sweep_Stim8hr.json` and `src/arbiter/lbd/propose.py:74-87` explicitly counts all classes before keeping supported variants.
findings.concern_path: The UI, narration, or transcript gate can silently omit a real verdict class. Worse, a future 'try any gene' interaction could surface `refuted_effect` and the app may badge it as generic NO or break if the enum is hardcoded from the plan.
findings.suggested_action: Make the demo-surface verdict taxonomy match the code: supported, supported_weak, supported_flagged, untested, refuted_for_c, refuted_effect, refuted_program, plus an explicit policy for any unreachable/reserved class.

findings.title: Option A is not actually zero-risk because the notebook contains a live public-S3 depth cell
findings.evidence: The fallback is described as 'zero new product scope' at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:55`, but the notebook imports `s3fs`/`h5py` and opens the public S3 DE file at `notebooks/pyzobot_arbiter_evidence_chain.ipynb:643-651`. The notebook itself notes the full 22,039-question sweep takes about 22 minutes and is loaded from cache at `notebooks/pyzobot_arbiter_evidence_chain.ipynb:16`.
findings.concern_path: Driving Jupyter live during recording can hang, fail on network, or spend the demo waiting on a remote 16.8 GB h5ad-backed slice. If the Streamlit UI slips and Option A is invoked late, the 'fallback' may become the highest timing risk.
findings.suggested_action: If Option A remains the fallback, pre-execute/cache all slow or network cells and drive only stable rendered outputs, or cut Scene 5 to the pre-rendered CS figure instead of running the S3 cell live.

findings.title: The funnel line overcompresses the actual cull and can sound like data-supported discovery inflation
findings.evidence: The narration says 'generated 22,039 — and the referee culled them to 30 it could back with a receipt at every hop' at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:86-87`. The repo's actual funnel is 3,935 A genes, 22,039 eligible gene-disease pairs, 43 disease-C-supported total, 30 clean full-chain supported, 10 weak, 3 flagged, 1 refuted_effect, and 21,995 refuted_for_c in `data/lbd_out/sweep_Stim8hr.json`; `src/arbiter/lbd/propose.py:115-120` defines those distinct counts.
findings.concern_path: The simplified line is close but brittle: it hides the literature/knownness gate and the 43-to-30 distinction. A reviewer may read '22,039 generated -> 30 backed' as the referee alone collapsing all generated questions to clean findings, while the repo says 30 is a joint novelty-gate plus clean full-chain subset.
findings.suggested_action: Narrate the exact funnel grain: '22,039 literature-eligible gene-disease questions; 43 had disease-C support; 30 were clean full-chain supported.'

findings.title: The depth narration risks overclaiming with 'definitively excluded' language
findings.evidence: The project requires calibrated language in `CLAUDE.md:13-16`, and AGENTS says never use uncalibrated language and prefer 'consistent with / re-derived / refuted / untested / flagged.' The plan's Scene 5 says the STAT6 confounder is 'excluded' at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:96-98`, while the writeup escalates to 'DEFINITIVELY REFUTED' / 'DEFINITIVELY EXCLUDED' at `docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md:80-97`.
findings.concern_path: Even if the cis-artifact check is strong, demo narration that says 'definitively excluded' can sound like proof-language in a biology claim. That cuts against the project's core trust contract and gives judges an avoidable overclaim to penalize under Depth/Impact.
findings.suggested_action: Use narrower receipt language on-screen and in voice: 'STAT6 does not move under NAB2 knockdown in the authors’ genome-wide DE table, so the cis-artifact explanation is refuted by this check.'

findings.title: The recommended UI path lacks a dependency/run preflight; `streamlit` is not in runtime deps
findings.evidence: The plan calls for `app/streamlit_app.py` at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:108-112`, but `requirements.txt:1-8` contains pandas, requests, h5py, and s3fs only; no Streamlit dependency or run command exists yet.
findings.concern_path: The UI may be quick to write but still fail on a clean machine, recording environment, or managed host. With five days left this is fixable, but calling the scope 'half day' without a dependency/server smoke test underestimates production risk.
findings.suggested_action: Add an explicit preflight milestone: dependency added, `streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py` launches from clean env, three showcased triples render, and Playwright can click them screen-only.

findings.title: The plan says 'type any gene→disease' but the product is atlas- and disease-list-bounded
findings.evidence: The recommendation says 'Type any gene→disease and watch a receipt-backed verdict resolve' at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:65-67`, but the build step correctly limits disease selection to the 12 eligible diseases at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:108-110`; `entities.load_c()` also returns only eligible disease names at `src/arbiter/lbd/entities.py:90-94`.
findings.concern_path: A broad 'any gene→disease' claim invites judges to expect general biology adjudication. The implementation is a deterministic referee over a specific Perturb-seq atlas, condition, and 12-disease C universe. Overselling the input space undermines the honest-referee brand.
findings.suggested_action: Phrase the interaction as 'choose a perturbation gene and one of the atlas-backed disease modules' rather than 'any gene→disease.'

findings.title: Streamlit + Playwright screen-only automation is treated as solved before selectors exist
findings.evidence: The demo-video harness requires real UI interactions only and forbids API/deep-link workarounds at `C:/Users/wijesingheds/.claude/skills/demo-video/SKILL.md:58-75`; its click helper relies on locators and visible controls at `C:/Users/wijesingheds/.claude/skills/demo-video/lib/harness.mjs:175-184`. No Streamlit app or stable test ids/selectors exist yet; the plan only says to verify manually at `docs/plans/demo_video_plan_2026-07-08.md:108-112`.
findings.concern_path: Streamlit reruns the page on widget changes and generates framework-managed DOM. Without deliberately stable labels and post-click wait conditions, the recording may intermittently click stale controls or capture loading/rerun states. This is a demo production risk, not an enterprise concern.
findings.suggested_action: Require stable visible labels for all controls, no hidden state dependency, and a one-command Playwright smoke script that performs the three showcased adjudications before video recording.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Claude must address the core contradiction first: if falsification is the product, why does the proposed 180s arc open with funnel and YES instead of a live UNTESTED/REFUTED verdict, and how will the UI truthfully render SATB1 as a one-hop gate failure rather than a fake four-hop chain?
